purpose: Separate high-confidence inferences from direct owner answers while drafting the remaining LMHG specialty pages.

confidence_method.high: The same decision appears across several owner answers, follows a confirmed parent-page rule, and does not create a new service claim.
confidence_method.medium: The decision is supported by current site copy and related owner answers, but the exact service boundary was not stated directly.
confidence_method.low: The decision could change service scope, billing, legal or clinical expectations, geographic eligibility, or SEO page separation. Owner review is needed before promotion.

sitewide_high_confidence_rules: Write in a plainspoken and practical voice at or below a 6th grade reading level.
Start with what the potential client or parent is seeing, not a clinical definition.
Use examples in short lists when they help the reader scan the page.
Explain that plans are individualized and may involve more than one LMHG provider when roles are clear.
Use the Reach Out intake form as the main next step and phone calls as a secondary option.
Mention the 7 to 14 day timing only with a caveat that specific provider requests can take longer.
Keep insurance language brief and route detailed questions to the insurance page.
Do not promise emergency response, outcomes, guaranteed resources, medication management, testing, legal opinions, or services LMHG does not provide.
Use one main search intent per page and link naturally to the parent or closely related service.

high_confidence_inferences: Speak mainly to parents while keeping the page readable for teens.
Use Teen Therapy as the main term and teen counseling or adolescent counseling as supporting terms.
Cover mood changes, shutdown, anger, school pressure, social strain, trauma, and family conflict.
Connect the page to Child Therapy and Family Therapy without treating teens like younger children.
low_confidence_items: The exact LMHG policy for teen privacy, parent updates, and safety-related disclosures has not been stated.

high_confidence_inferences: Use Adult Counseling as the main term and adult therapy as a supporting term.
Carry forward the Individual Counseling page's privacy, choice, broad concern list, team approach, and payment rules.
Write for adults with work, family, grief, stress, relationship, trauma, and life-change concerns.
Use Anxiety and Depression Therapy as the main term while using counseling naturally in body copy.
Speak to symptoms and daily effects without diagnosing the reader.
Keep the page within therapy scope and do not imply psychiatry or medication management.
Use the Individual Counseling team and payment rules.
The current LMHG page belongs under Family Therapy and focuses on caregiver-child trust, connection, emotional safety, and repair.
Caregiver participation is important and the plan should be individualized.
The page should distinguish this work from Family Reunification and should not imply custody work or guaranteed repair.
low_confidence_items: Current Google results lean toward adult attachment patterns and relationship therapy, while LMHG's current page is parent-child and family focused. The intended public scope needs confirmation.

high_confidence_inferences: Speak to parents who see behavior but may not know the cause.
Present Child Behavioral Therapy as one type of Child Therapy chosen from the presenting problem.
Include parent check-ins, home routines, family sessions, and school coordination when useful.
Exclude psychiatry, medication, autism or learning testing, ABA, speech therapy, occupational therapy, custody opinions, and crisis response.
low_confidence_items: LMHG has not yet confirmed whether the service should publicly be called Child Behavioral Therapy or Child Behavioral Intervention.
The exact use of written behavior plans, reward systems, school observation, or parent-only coaching has not been stated.

high_confidence_inferences: Speak mainly to Medicaid clients.
Define a Community Support Associate as a therapeutic assistant who helps the client practice therapy goals in daily life.
State that the client still sees a therapist and that the Community Support Associate works closely with that therapist.
Distinguish this service from Case Management, which focuses more on plans, resources, systems, and coordination.
The owner already confirmed that Conflict Resolution can include larger family systems, including parents and grandparents and blended families, so the page should not be couples-only.
Include couples, parent-child conflict, siblings, parents and grandparents, and blended family members when they fit the presenting conflict.
Focus on repeating conflict, escalation, shutdown, repair, and making hard talks more structured.
State clearly that this is therapy work, not mediation, legal work, or a service that takes sides.
Use Conflict Resolution Counseling as the main public phrase because the broader Louisville SERP supports counseling intent while the current couples-only phrase leans toward mediation.
low_confidence_items: The current slug and title are couples-only and do not match the confirmed broader family scope. A redirect and final replacement slug require an architecture decision before promotion.

high_confidence_inferences: Speak to parents and caregivers dealing with behavior, boundaries, communication, stress, and home routines.
Use an individualized mix of caregiver guidance, family sessions, child therapy, and school coordination when useful.
Do not frame the service as a parenting class unless LMHG actually offers a class.
low_confidence_items: It is not yet clear whether Parenting Support may be delivered as parent-only counseling, coaching, family therapy, or a case-by-case mix for billing and public labeling.

high_confidence_inferences: Speak to parents and caregivers.
Explain that children often use play to show and work through feelings they cannot yet explain in a long talk.
Present Play Therapy as one type of Child Therapy and explain that parent involvement often grows as the child gets younger.
Cover big feelings, behavior changes, anxiety, grief, trauma, school stress, and family change without promising a result.
Current site structure places the page under Couples Counseling.
A workable distinction is to focus this page on trust, closeness, repeating patterns, and relationship decisions while the conflict page focuses on arguments and repair skills.
Keep the parent-page rule that married and unmarried couples of any gender are welcome.
low_confidence_items: It is not confirmed whether Relationship Counseling is only for romantic couples or may also cover other close relationships.
Search intent strongly overlaps Couples Counseling, so the final page purpose and keyword target need owner confirmation.

high_confidence_inferences: Define in-home therapy as therapy delivered in the client's home, not a different therapy method.
Use in-home mental health therapy early because search results are mixed with physical therapy and home health.
The service may be useful when transportation, home routines, child needs, or family patterns make office-only therapy less useful.
Medicaid and commercial insurance may cover the service depending on fit and benefits.
low_confidence_items: The exact service area, client eligibility rules, and any home-safety requirements have not been stated.
